content.problemStatement:
Q5) A . Differentiate between class objects, instance objects, and their attributes
& methods. How does attribute resolution work when accessing an attribute from an instance?   --- 4 Marks.
constraints: The explanation should cover the basics of object-oriented programming.
The explanation should include attribute resolution in instance objects.
content.outputFormat: Print a clear, concise explanation of the differences between class objects, instance objects, and their attributes and methods, and describe how attribute resolution works when accessing an attribute from an instance.

sampleTestCases.output: Class objects are templates that define attributes and methods. Instance objects are concrete objects created from a class; they have their own attribute values but share the class's methods. When an attribute is accessed on an instance, the lookup order is: instance namespace, then the class, then base classes following the method resolution order (MRO).
sampleTestCases.explanation: The program prints the required explanation because there is no input.

When an attribute is accessed on an instance, the lookup order is: instance namespace, then the class, then base classes following the method resolution order (MRO).

hidden.label: Case 5
hidden.expectedOutput: Class objects are templates that define attributes and methods. Instance objects are concrete objects created from a class; they have their own attribute values but share the class's methods. When an attribute is accessed on an instance, the lookup order is: instance namespace, then the class, then base classes following the method resolution order (MRO).

solution.approach: The program does not need to process any input. It simply prints a predefined explanation that covers: • Class objects – the blueprint defining attributes and methods. • Instance objects – concrete objects created from the class, holding their own attribute values while sharing methods. • Attributes and methods – data stored in the class or instance and functions bound to them. • Attribute resolution – when an attribute is accessed on an instance, Python (or similar OOP language) first looks in the instance’s __dict__, then in the class, then in base classes following the method resolution order (MRO). This static text satisfies the requirement.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) – the program only prints a constant‑size string.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few constant‑size variables are used.

code.JavaScript: const output = \"Class objects are templates that define attributes and methods. Instance objects are concrete objects created from a class; they have their own attribute values but share the class's methods. When an attribute is accessed on an instance, the lookup order is: instance namespace, then the class, then base classes following the method resolution order (MRO).\";\nconsole.log(output);
